key_insight: DSGNAR is a scalable second-order optimization framework that makes PINN training far better conditioned, enabling much higher accuracy and faster convergence than standard methods.
engineering_relevance: It could make physics-informed neural networks practical for engineering PDE solving where classical solvers are expensive or PINNs previously failed to reach sufficient precision.
method: Uses a doubly-sketched Gauss-Newton update to approximate second-order curvature efficiently.
Adds Adaptive Ratio control to tune regularization and step length during training.
Targets ill-conditioned PINN loss landscapes across nonlinear, chaotic, multi-scale, high-dimensional, and Navier-Stokes problems.
evidence_signal: Across a suite of benchmark PDEs, the abstract reports relative \ell_2 errors as low as 3×10^-16 in double precision, five orders of magnitude improvement on Burgers' equation, eight orders on a high-dimensional Poisson problem, and fast single-precision convergence to 4.75×10^-7 in under ten seconds.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate curvature approximation and careful hyperparameter/precision handling, so memory, implementation complexity, and stability should be checked on new architectures and PDEs.
paper_title: An Optimisation Framework for the Well-Conditioned Training of Physics-Informed Neural Networks
